pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh:13 and export_result.sh:8 both explicitly reference this path
Code analysis: setup_task.sh:63 and export_result.sh:9 both explicitly reference this path
Verified against official Jitsi Meet documentation and env.example. ENABLE_AUTH=1 enables authentication in Jitsi Meet

Verified against official Jitsi Meet documentation (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/docker-jitsi-meet/master/env.example). AUTH_TYPE=internal is one of four valid options (internal, jwt, ldap, matrix)
Verified against official Jitsi Meet documentation and env.example. ENABLE_GUESTS=1 enables guest access with authentication
Code analysis: setup_task.sh:53 unregisters 'admin@meet.jitsi', export_result.sh:70 checks for 'admin.dat' file in prosody
Code analysis: docker-compose.yml:28 sets XMPP_DOMAIN=meet.jitsi, setup_task.sh:53 explicitly uses meet.jitsi
Code analysis: task.json and task description explicitly reference this URL. Valid Jitsi Meet URL format - any URL pattern http://localhost:8080/<roomname> creates/joins a room

privileged_info_summary: Task configures Jitsi Meet version stable-9753 to require authentication for moderators while allowing guest access. Environment variables ENABLE_AUTH=1, AUTH_TYPE=internal, and ENABLE_GUESTS=1 must be set in /home/ga/jitsi/.env. A moderator user 'admin' on domain 'meet.jitsi' must be registered in the Prosody XMPP server using docker exec with prosodyctl. Docker containers must be restarted to apply configuration changes. Task verification navigates to http://localhost:8080/SecureDomainTest and checks for login prompt visibility via VLM analysis.
